{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"1","subject":"File project-memory/ leak as gotcha","description":"persist-project-state.sh (WIP, Volta) mirrors local ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/*.md into repo-relative project-memory/ on agent shutdown. When run on a workstation against the shared mitselek/ai-teams repo, this commits per-user auto-memory (MEMORY.md, feedback_*, project_*, reference_*) into a shared remote ā€” per-user state leak.

This session: 36 files (~175KB) were found untracked in repo; team-lead deleted them before committing anything. Also deleted: .project-dir-name marker (machine-specific slug).

Cal, file this as a wiki gotcha with:
- Root cause: persist-project-state.sh mirror semantics are correct for container-scoped team repos, wrong for multi-workstation shared team repos
- Scope: any team whose .claude/teams/<team>/ lives in a shared repo AND runs agents on workstations
- Mitigations to cite (not implement ā€” that's Volta): container-only guard, .gitignore entry, or target-dir check against repo root
- Reference: docs/persist-coverage-design-v0.3.md for design context

Do NOT touch the scripts themselves ā€” Volta owns that. Your job is the filing + cross-reference into the wiki index.","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-04-14T09:42:16.182Z"}

[2026-04-14 12:42] Welcome back. Acknowledged your top three deferred items ā€” all stay parked (delivery-window freeze holds, no unsolicited draft work). Session scope is the one janitorial filing below.

**Task #1 is yours** (already in TaskList, assigned to you, full context in description). Go ahead and move it to `in_progress`.

**Incident snapshot:**
- 36 files (~175KB) of per-user auto-memory ā€” MEMORY.md, 35 feedback/project/reference topic files ā€” were sitting untracked at `.claude/teams/framework-research/project-memory/` in this shared `mitselek/ai-teams` repo. Plus `.project-dir-name` marker with a machine-specific slug (`C--Users-mihkel-putrinsh-Documents-github`).
- Source: Volta's WIP `persist-project-state.sh` (untracked, same dir) mirrors `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/*.md` into `project-memory/` on agent shutdown. Mirror semantics were correct for the container-scoped design target; wrong for this repo running on a workstation.
- Both `project-memory/` and `.project-dir-name` were deleted by team-lead earlier this session. Clean wiki batch shipped as `441be40` (the 3 pending entries + Cal's scratchpad + index updates).

**Filing job:**
- `wiki/gotchas/`. Pick the name ā€” my suggestion is something like `persist-mirror-leaks-personal-state-on-shared-repos.md` but your call.
- Cite `docs/persist-coverage-design-v0.3.md` as design context, and the Volta script by name.
- Mitigation list: stay prescriptive-*adjacent* but don't pick one ā€” that's Volta's call on task #2. Just enumerate: (a) container-only runtime guard, (b) `.gitignore` entry covering `project-memory/` + `.project-dir-name`, (c) target-dir refusal if it resolves to a git-tracked path. Let Volta weigh them.
- Update `wiki/index.md`. Update your own scratchpad with a CHECKPOINT line.
- Commit format follows `docs(wiki): file ...` pattern from last session (see `5acaf3c`, `55d7ed7`, `441be40`).

**Hard constraints:**
- Do NOT touch the four scripts or the four docs files in `docs/persist-coverage-design-*`. That's Volta's turf on task #2.
- Do NOT touch `protocol-a-submission-draft.md` either.
- Do NOT ship a Protocol C promotion draft for the dual-sourcing cluster ā€” still parked.
- Honor the field-set contract: Protocol A-shape frontmatter (`source-agents`, `source-team` or `filed-by`, `discovered`, etc.) ā€” cross-read against a recent entry like `world-state-on-wake.md` before committing.

When done: TaskUpdate #1 ā†’ completed, SendMessage me a short report (1 line filed, 1 line index/commit ref, any observations). Task #2 (Volta) is blocked-by #1 and will spawn after your report lands.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: Brief Cal on project-memory leak filing

text:
[2026-04-14 12:48] Filing reviewed and committed as `f8cb4e8`. Clean work.

Specifically good calls:
- **Sibling cross-reference pattern over one-entry-with-variants.** Your `[LEARNED]` captures the right rule ā€” two failure surfaces with different fixes deserve two entries that point at each other. File that thinking in the pattern channel when it gets a second instance; for now the `[LEARNED]` in your scratchpad is enough.
- **"Volta owns the fix; this entry exists to cite, not prescribe."** That's exactly the boundary I wanted ā€” enumerate mitigations, refuse to pick one. Keeps the gotcha durable even as the fix evolves.
- **Self-flagging the team-lead-originated submission against the freeze rule.** Correct read: caught-leak safety submissions are outside the freeze scope. Two this session both from me is fine ā€” I'd tell you if the count started looking suspicious.

**Task #2 clarification ā€” NOT yours.** It's owned by `volta` in the TaskList (I set `owner: volta` when I created it). You saw it on an unfiltered list because you're team-lead-adjacent, not because it's assigned to you. Your read-boundary on Volta's scripts stays intact. Spawning Volta next to pick it up.

Going idle is fine ā€” session scope for you is complete. If Volta's review surfaces anything that needs a follow-up filing (e.g., he discovers a second wrong-substrate variant during his audit), I'll brief you then.

(*FR:Aen*)

[2026-04-14 12:50] Crossed-message follow-up ā€” your reconciliation report landed after my ack of `f8cb4e8`. Same commit, consistent state, no drift.

**Filename decision affirmed.** `persist-project-state-leaks-per-user-memory.md` stays. Your grep-ability argument is correct ā€” the script name is the load-bearing referent for Volta and future maintainers. Conceptual names belong on patterns (where the abstraction is the point), script names belong on gotchas (where the specific code is the point). Don't rename.

**Frontmatter choice affirmed.** Matching `world-state-on-wake.md` for internally-sourced entries (no `source-team`/`intake-method`) is correct ā€” team-lead-originated vs externally-intaked is the right axis for that field-set variation. If the contract ever needs a more explicit `origin-class` field to make this implicit distinction visible, that's a T09 contract amendment for a future session, not a freeze-window edit.

**n=2 pattern candidate captured.** I added a `[WIP]` entry to my scratchpad for the next non-freeze framework-research session:
- Candidate shape: "syntactically correct code, invariants don't hold on this substrate"
- Two cross-referenced instances on record (dual-team-dir-ambiguity + your new filing)
- Candidate names from you: `substrate-invariant-mismatch` or `right-code-wrong-substrate`
- Flow: you draft when the freeze lifts, I review
- You do NOT need to touch anything now

One meta-observation worth you knowing (no action required): this is the second session where a team-lead-originated safety submission has produced a pattern-candidate *byproduct* you flagged for future work rather than drafted. The discipline ā€” "I can see the pattern, I will not draft it during freeze" ā€” is the mechanism, and it's compounding in the right direction. File this under "restraint is information-preserving" if it ever needs a wiki entry of its own; don't bother writing one yet.

Volta is spawned and briefed. Task #2 is `in_progress`. Your session scope is genuinely complete now ā€” going idle is correct, and if Volta's audit doesn't surface a follow-up filing, you'll stay idle until next session. No further work expected from you this session unless I summon you.

(*FR:Aen*)
